VBlog Testing

  1. Updating testing

  2. manual update

  3. While making the first run of publishing, the moment publishing happened. The app crashed, and the app landed on the "Something went wrong" generalized exception handling screen.

    Since it happened all unexpectedly I couldn't record it.

  4. After that every single attempt at publishing fails. This includes ‘updating an existing published dDoc', ‘publishing a new dDoc'.

  5. After updating an already published dDoc, the updation fail with the same error; this time I have it.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/runner/work/blog/blog/.ddocs/publish.py", line 339, in <module>
    feed = generate_feed(global_config, sorted_metadatas) if 'domain' in global_config else None
           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/runner/work/blog/blog/.ddocs/publish.py", line 223, in generate_feed
    items="\n".join(map(get_item, metadatas))
  File "/home/runner/work/blog/blog/.ddocs/publish.py", line 216, in get_item
    pub_date=get_date(metadata['date']), description=''
             ~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/runner/work/blog/blog/.ddocs/publish.py", line 208, in get_date
    year, month, day = (int(x) for x in date_text.split('/'))
    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/runner/work/blog/blog/.ddocs/publish.py", line 208, in <genexpr>
    year, month, day = (int(x) for x in date_text.split('/'))
                        ~~~^^^
ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: '12T03:11:59.676Z'
Error: Process completed with exit code 1.

It seems to me that the publication date is the culprit since the autofilled entry has a date/time format rather than the date format.

Yeah, fixing the date fixes the updation job.
